transcript: Hey, this is Humphrey. My name’s Humphrey Finger.
And I’m waitin’ for a pizza up here.
Yeah. It’s— I think it’s under Buford Finger up ‘ere! [delay]Whadda you guys got goin’ on up ‘ere? [exhales]
Shall I just pass the mic around, see if anybody wants to ask you a question or two?—
Anybody wants to ask a question…
Alright, just come up— come on up and… Yeah.
Outstanding. I won’t take up too much of your time—I just have a bit of a question for ya.
What is the meaning of life?
U— Oh, we got cut off, there. I didn’t hear the question. No, um…
Um, I said, uh, “What’s the meaning of life?”
[laughs] I’m just kidding. Uh… Shucks, buddy!
Ya know? It’s not a universal answer, I’m pretty convinced; it varies from individual to individual up ‘ere. [exhales] [sniffs]
Um… I’ll tell ya, let me—
Lemme get some accompaniment with that.
Hey, you’ve reached the voicemail address for Andrew.
I’m unable to take your call at the moment, so please leave your name, number, and a brief message. I’ll be sure to call you back. Thank you.—
I’m gonna— I’m gonna marinate on that a little bit. I’m gonna marinate, and then, frankly, I’m gonna urinate a little later in the hour, but…
Thanks for asking, though. I’ll get back to ya up ‘ere! [exhales]
Uh, I have a… question, uh, that… I was wondering about the— the Gomez call. She said you’d been—
You’d b— You’d been callin’ all the time. Can you tella little bit about the… like, the stuff we didn’t hear before that— the one call that is on the album.

The Gomez? Ya know, there’s a misconception that I spent like a year… or a long time calling a wrong number and—ya know—setting it up for the time where I finally called as myself. And that’s not true. It was spontaneous, it was improvised. I looked at the phone book under “Gomez” and that’s what happened. So… It was just a… spur of the moment up ‘ere! [delay, pitch-shifted]And that’s… really all there was to it, ya know?
And that lady and I are now married. So there ya go.
No, I appreciate it! I appreciate where you’re comin’ from up— up ‘ere![pitch-shifted]I understand. I can take— I can understand where you’re coin’ from up ‘ere!—
[sample of children singing a voicemail parody of “Party In The U.S.A.” by Miley Cyrus]
Just looking out at the audience, seeing it anyone else has any questions.—
…lookin’ around like yeah!So leave a message after the tone—we’ll call you back anyway!Yeah, yeah! Yeah—yeah!Leave a message anyway!
’Kay that’s great, oh yeah, oh sure, uh-huh! [delay, pitch-shifted]
At the tone, please record your message![beep]
Hey, uh, uh, what’s pipin’, LPC? I was just, uh—
I was just wonderin’ how you originally got into contact with, uh, Mr. Trebek—uh, how you originally got that number.

Yeah, I’ve kind of, uh, stopped. I’ve been tempted to, uh, wish him well— [sniffs] —lately with his recent prognosis.But, uh… It was just another one of those things; it was given to me by a person… and I called it… ya know, a little unsure of its validity, but as soon as he answered—and I mean instantaneously—I knew it was him. And, ya know, that was—God!—10 years ago already, so…And he’s kept his phone—he’s kept it on, he answers it.Ya know, frick it! Let’s call him right now! Here we go! [laughs]
Okay. I screwed it up. I’ll have to figure it out. Maybe if anybody else has a question, I can answer it while I’m trying to figure out what I did wrong.
Hey! How’s it goin’, man? Uh…—
—I was wo— Uh, I’m sorry, go ‘head?
I was thankin’ you—that’s all! Oh, yeah! Uh-huh! [delay]
Oh, yeah! Alright, alright, sounds good!—
Uh, [stammering] Yeah, okay!— Uh… I wa— Yeah, I—
Yeah, this is, uh, Percival. How ya doin’, sir?
Oh, good. I— I got a new alto… uh…
Is that too loud or is it disturbing anyone, disrupting anyone up ‘ere?
Or is it acceptable level? I’m new to the area!Oh, he hung up! Okay! Alex is gone. Please continue with your question up ‘ere! [delay]
Alright, we’ve— we’ve got— we’ve got somebody else coming up for some time with you, LPC. Here you go.—
I have a— I have another number… to try.
Oh, totally! Okay. What’s… uh… what’s that number?
Uh, it’s my boss that just fired me.
Oh, okay, well good. Let’s… uh… let’s do it. [sniffs]
Phil. Okay, it’s ringing. Okay, his name’s Phil. Got it.
Yeah, this is Percival with ATT. Uh…—
Some questions about your account, here.
We’ve been noticing… some excessive usage to 900 numbers—976 numbers. To that particular exchange. Uh… So we have—
—instituted a block. You’ll have to, uh…
—pay extra… for those types of calls… uh, going forward.—
Wait, wait, what—? Hold on a second. For—
—what phone number are you referring to?
All phone numbers associated with your account.
Okay. I haven’t been making any outgoing calls to a 9-7-6 number.
I hear that all the time. [inhales] Uh… so we have restricted those types of exchanges—900, 976 numbers. Uh…—
—are gonna be blocked, alright? So I don’t—
What i—? What is a “9-7-6 number”?
—lotta late-night phone numbers; things of that nature. I don’t know if you have, uh…—
—someone else in the house who’s making the calls. Um…
No. It’s just… my phone and my wife’s phone.
’Kay, maybe she’s making the 900 number calls? Maybe I need to talk to her?—
I don’t think that that— she would be the one making those calls.

Okay, so it is you making those calls? Alright, well there is a block on there; if you wanna reverse, that is a one-time charge of a hundred and twenty-nine dollars. Uh… We can just do that by credit card, Master Charge or Visa, if you’d like to…
[arpeggiated synth tones and white noise, continual]

Okay. Well then we’re gonna leave the block on there and, uh… If you do attempt any more of those calls we will block local calls as well. So, I’d advise you to discontinue that… uh… at once… pretty much.
Thank you. That was Phil, everybody! I’ve had my fill of Phil, to be frank with ya.How’s everybody doin’ up ‘ere?! [delay, pitch-shifted]
Woo! Well god bless Alec [sic] Trebek, I tell ya what.
The guy just picked up, ya know? What a guy! It’s amazing.
Any— any other queries, uh… any commentary, any—?
[ special information tone into generic TTS message plays]
We’re sorry! You have reached a number that has been disconnected or is no longer in service.
Hey, um… [clears throat] How did you get the lead for the, uh, Super Nintendo call and… how many years did you call NBS Electronics for?
Okay, so the… Super Nintendo? What was the other part… you said?
Uh, how many years did you spend calling NBS Electronics?

Well, I shopped there! I bought a Sonic Maximizer there—a BBE—and I bought some cables there. And the guy has, like, uh, target [delay]Like a dart board, uh… with… uh, Bill Clinton’s face on it. There’s, like, literally, bullet holes in it. So the guy’s— the guy’s harsh. And then, just one day, I was living in Los Angeles and I was like, “I’m gonna call that guy! I haven’t seen him in years.” And then boom! The call that resulted was, uh… we started talking ‘bout Julien Assange. [delay]And, uh… my suspicions were correct—he hasn’t changed. He’s… hardcore… uh… Rightwing. And so, since then, I’ve only had, like, a few lengthy conversations. ‘Cause now—you saw what happened—he’s onto it. And, uh… other people have picken [sic] up the torch. [delay, pitch-shifted]So… Yeah, I’ve— I’ve limited my time with Fat Willie—that’s his name.
How did you find out about—? How did you find out about Sissios Puffios?

Oh, I think that was from Buford… hit me up to that. Buford’s that guy—he works at Alpha Beta. Dude’s absolutely deranged! [exhales]And he’s a black cowboy. And… I was like, “Show me what ya got! [exhales]”
The mailbox is full and cannot accept any messages at this time. Goodbye!—

Well I didn’t wanna frickin’ talk to ya anyway, buttcheek!So I hope I answered your question. Now, w— we bonded over, uh… Amon Düül records. Well if it isn’t.. The Falcon & The Whip! [exhales]Whipp-ahh! [imitates whip-cracking noise]. Yeah.
Okay, um, I got a question for you about the Coffin Shop call. Um…—

Yeah! I just wanted to know if you could tell me any, like— anything weird or anything special about that. It was the first one I showed to my dad who’s— I’m Armenian, he’s super Armenian, all the names that you use in it are really Armenian—
And my dad’s like, “Why are you showing me this man who harasses Armenian people all the time?
Um… [breaks up, perhaps, “And he needs to…”] learn that it’s, uh… no one’s, um… they’re not the privileged ones. [unclear]—
Yeah, I was wondering if you could help me out about it.
I thought that “Garibaldians” were fish! Are they Armenian fish? Is that what you’re telling me?
No. Like, “Sarcagian”, “Garibaldian”—it’s just, like, very Armenian-sounding last names. I didn’t know if there was any background on that. [unclear]—
It’s a very well-known Armenian fish—very large!
Okay, yeah, and the business card— It was just a business card that was given to me and it says, “Coffin it up!”

“Coffin it up!” it says on there, so I gave her a holler up ‘ere [quietly] real quick!And that just, uh… really the long-and-short of it. It was just improvisation, uh… at a coffin shop! “Alligator Monday” has become my favorite euphemism for death… uh… since then, but, uh… [laughs]—
[unclear]. That was back when I was a Conquistador—
Things were a lot different back then, when I was a Conquistador… Uh…

I was sorta involved in the Indie Matador circuit. Um… I chased dogs around ’n piglets—that kinda stuff—until I graduated to, uh… sort of Indie Conquistador. Got my cape, got my, uh… eye mask, and then I just went around the neighborhood… at that stage! You can appreciate that, right? I’m sure you can appreciate that.Listening to a lot of Ian MacKaye for inspiration, at that stage.
Hi, uh… ”How’s the weather up there?” [laughs]

[pitch-shifted] Uh, it’s chilly! It’s chilly— It’s warm in the, um… It’s—it’s warm— It’s warm in the… afternoon! It’s—it’s, like, cold in the morning and then it’s warm in the afternoon. Then it’s cold at night again—it’s, like, fluctuating! Alll day long! Fluctuating all night long!
[delay, pitch-shifted]Alll day long! Fluctuating all night long!—
Fluctuating all the time. So I guess that means the seasons are changing.
[delay, pitch-shifted]—Alll day long! Fluctuating all night long!——Fluctuating all the time. So I guess that means the seasons are changing.—
[quiet] Uh, do you have any—? Do you have any pets?—
So I guess that means the seasons are changing—big time!''[delay, pitch-shifted] Oh! big time! Big time! Ohh! Major fluctuations! Oh, fluctuating wil’! [delay, pitch-shifted]—
Yeah, you know what I mean. How’s it goin’ up ‘ere?! [exhales] I’m tryin’ to keep up. I’m bundled up right now.
It’s cold, man! You got any pets?—
Frickin’-A! I’ll tell ya it’s cold! Woo!
Uh… I have a… 60’s Fender Jaguar that I call “Bluey”! ‘Cause It’s a custom color—Lake Placid Blue—and I call it Bluey.
Hello! You’ve reached the voice message mailbox for Bob Agra number two! Please leave me your name and number—
—and I will get back to you as—
I think that’s it! Yeah, Bluey. That’s the only one that has a name.
So, LPC, looks like we’re—we’re about ready to wrap up because your, uh… your, uh… world premiere screening—
Hello! Please state your name after the tone—
—and Google Voice will try to connect you. [tone]
Gosh, I just wanna thank everybody so much for being there and, uh… and— as well as Spectacle for hosting this and everybody for being there. Thank you! I wanna thank you very much! Thanks a lot!
Thanks, LPC! We’ll— we’ll talk soon!
